sn2.23:1.1: As he was standing there, Serī the deva’s son recited these verses in the Blessed One’s presence:
sn2.23:3.1: The Buddha: “Those who give it through conviction,
sn2.23:4.2: one who conquers [that] stain, should give a gift.
sn2.23:4.3: Deeds of merit are the support for beings
sn2.23:4.4: when they arise in the other world.”
sn2.23:5.2: How well this was stated by the Lord Blessed One!”
sn2.23:7.2: one who conquers [that] stain, should give a gift.
sn2.23:7.3: Deeds of merit are the support for beings
sn2.23:7.4: when they arise in the other world.’”
sn2.23:8.1: “In the past, lord, I was a king named Serī—a donor, a lord of giving, one who spoke in praise of giving.
sn2.23:8.2: At my four gates I had gifts given to contemplatives & brahmans, poor people & travelers, mendicants & beggars.
sn2.23:8.3: “Then my harem women came to me and said, ‘Your majesty, you give gifts, but we don’t give gifts.
sn2.23:8.4: It would be good if, depending on you, we gave gifts & did deeds of merit.’
sn2.23:8.5: “Then the thought occurred to me, ‘Here I am, a donor, a lord of giving, one who speaks in praise of giving.
sn2.23:8.7: “Let us give gifts,” what am I to say?’
sn2.23:8.9: So I gave the first gate to the harem women.
sn2.23:8.10: There the harem women gave gifts, and my gifts returned to me.
sn2.23:9.1: “Then my noble-warrior subordinates came to me and said,
sn2.23:9.2: ‘Your majesty, you give gifts, the harem women give gifts, but we don’t give gifts.
sn2.23:9.3: It would be good if, depending on you, we gave gifts & did deeds of merit.’
sn2.23:9.5: ‘Here I am, a donor, a lord of giving, one who speaks in praise of giving.
sn2.23:9.6: So when they say, “Let us give gifts,” what am I to say?’
sn2.23:9.7: So I gave the second gate to my noble-warrior subordinates.
sn2.23:9.8: There the noble-warrior subordinates gave gifts, and my gifts returned to me.
sn2.23:10.1: “Then my troops came to me and said,
sn2.23:10.2: ‘Your majesty, you give gifts, the harem women give gifts, the noble-warrior subordinates give gifts, but we don’t give gifts.
sn2.23:10.3: It would be good if, depending on you, we gave gifts & did deeds of merit.’
sn2.23:10.5: ‘Here I am, a donor, a lord of giving, one who speaks in praise of giving.
sn2.23:10.6: So when they say, “Let us give gifts,” what am I to say?’
sn2.23:10.7: So I gave the third gate to my troops.
sn2.23:10.8: There the troops gave gifts, and my gifts returned to me.
sn2.23:11.1: “Then brahmans & householders came to me and said,
sn2.23:11.2: ‘Your majesty, you give gifts, the harem women give gifts, the noble-warrior subordinates give gifts, the troops give gifts, but we don’t give gifts.
sn2.23:11.3: It would be good if, depending on you, we gave gifts & did deeds of merit.’
sn2.23:11.5: ‘Here I am, a donor, a lord of giving, one who speaks in praise of giving.
sn2.23:11.6: So when they say, “Let us give gifts,” what am I to say?’
sn2.23:11.7: So I gave the fourth gate to the brahmans & householders.
sn2.23:11.8: There the brahmans & householders gave gifts, and my gifts returned to me.
sn2.23:12.1: “Then my men came to me and said,
sn2.23:12.2: ‘Now your majesty isn’t giving any gifts.’
sn2.23:12.3: “When that was said, I said to my men,
sn2.23:12.4: ‘In that case, I say, bring half of the revenue generated in the outlying districts into the city, and give half of it as gifts to contemplatives & brahmans, poor people & travelers, mendicants & beggars.’
sn2.23:12.5: “And so, lord, in the meritorious deeds I did for such a long time, in the skillful deeds I did for such a long time,
sn2.23:12.6: I didn’t reach a limit of ‘so much merit’ or ‘so much fruit of merit’ or ‘so long I should stay in heaven.’
sn2.23:12.8: How well this was stated by the lord Blessed One!”
sn2.23:14.2: one who conquers [that] stain, should give a gift.
sn2.23:14.3: Deeds of merit are the support for beings
sn2.23:14.4: when they arise in the other world.’”
